description: Identified parallel execution opportunities for Capacium Phase 2

opportunities.name: Phase 2 workstreams parallel execution
opportunities.description: 4 workstreams (BUNDLE, ADAPTERS, LOCK, REGISTRY) have no cross-dependencies and can run in parallel after PHASE-INIT completes.

opportunities.name: Adapter tasks within WS-ADAPTERS
opportunities.description: ADAPTER-001 (Claude Code) and ADAPTER-002 (Gemini CLI) can be written in parallel since they are independent implementations of the same interface.

opportunities.name: Bundle tasks within WS-BUNDLE
opportunities.description: BUNDLE-003 (removal) and BUNDLE-004 (list/search/fingerprint) can start after BUNDLE-001 (manifest) without waiting for BUNDLE-002 (install).
opportunities.note: BUNDLE-002 remains on critical path; parallel tasks have some float

opportunities.name: SWBUNDLE + integration prep
opportunities.description: SWBUNDLE-002 (E2E verify) and INTEG-002 (docs) can run in parallel since they touch different systems.

opportunities.name: Registry design then build
opportunities.description: REGISTRY-001 (OpenAPI spec) and REGISTRY-002 (REST client) are sequential within the stream. No further parallelism within WS-REGISTRY.

bottlenecks: BUNDLE-002 (recursive installation) — on critical path, blocks SWBUNDLE
INTEG-001 (cross-stream tests) — must wait for all 4 workstreams + SWBUNDLE
recommendation: Execute all 4 workstreams in parallel (PAR-001). Within WS-ADAPTERS, parallelize ADAPTER-001 and ADAPTER-002 (PAR-002). Within WS-BUNDLE, parallelize BUNDLE-003 and BUNDLE-004 after BUNDLE-001 completes (PAR-003).
